summary: The diff implements both REQ-12 (backend claimAdmin bootstrap) and REQ-13 (frontend Claim Admin button on Admin Dashboard). The backend adds a migration module initializing adminPrincipal as null, and main.mo includes the adminPrincipal state variable. The frontend AdminDashboard exposes a Claim Admin button with proper flow, and useQueries.ts includes a useClaimAdmin mutation. However, the backend claimAdmin function implementation itself is truncated and cannot be fully verified, particularly the anonymous principal rejection, the one-shot enforcement, and continued enforcement of existing admin-gated functions. The frontend logic appears largely complete based on the summary and visible code.

missing_requirements.requirement: Anonymous callers are rejected by claimAdmin and receive an error.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend main.mo diff is truncated and the actual claimAdmin function body is not visible. There is no evidence in the visible diff that an anonymous principal check is performed in claimAdmin.

missing_requirements.requirement: A second call to claimAdmin from any principal returns an error and does not change adminPrincipal (one-shot enforcement).
missing_requirements.reason: The backend main.mo diff is truncated; the claimAdmin function implementation is not visible to confirm one-shot rejection logic is present.

missing_requirements.requirement: When the backend adminPrincipal is unset/unclaimed, the Admin Dashboard shows a 'Claim Admin' button instead of 'Access Denied' — specifically conditioned on the backend returning a designated 'unclaimed' status.
missing_requirements.reason: The visible AdminDashboard code shows the Claim Admin button when isAdmin is false and claimDenied is false, but there is no evidence of a distinct 'unclaimed' status returned from the backend being checked. The button is shown for all non-admin authenticated users, not only when the backend signals 'unclaimed'.

hallucinated_features.description: A migration module (backend/migration.mo) was added to handle state migration from an old actor shape to a new shape including adminPrincipal.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention any migration module or data migration concern — it only requires adding a claimAdmin bootstrap mechanism. Adding a full migration.mo with OldActor/NewActor type definitions goes beyond what was requested.

hallucinated_features.description: The AdminDashboard imports and uses a Variant_success_alreadyClaimed constant from the backend to discriminate the claimAdmin result.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not specify the shape of the claimAdmin return type or that it uses a named variant with 'alreadyClaimed'. This specific discriminated union design is an implementation detail not described in the requirements.
